zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Cecily Beaumont於1504年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾奧爾頓 奧 馮 希爾時，她的父親Sir William I De Beaumont 2nd Viscount, KN26歲，母親Mary Bassett34歲。她於1530年與Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1574年在英國倫敦享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Cecily Beaumont于1504年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾奧爾頓 奧 馮 希爾时，她的父亲Sir William I De Beaumont 2nd Viscount, KN26岁，母亲Mary Bassett34岁。她于1530年与Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire在爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1574年在英国倫敦享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Cecily
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cecilie
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cicely
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Cecily Beaumont was born in 1504, in Orton on the Hill, Leicestershire, England, her father, Sir William I De Beaumont 2nd Viscount, KN, was 26 and her mother, Mary Bassett, was 34. She married Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire in 1530, in Cork, County Cork, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1574, in London, England, at the age of 70.

fr.Orton on the Hill, Leicestershire, Engla: Orton on the Hill, Leicestershire, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Cecily Beaumont est née en 1504, à Orton on the Hill, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William I De Beaumont 2nd Viscount, KN, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mary Bassett, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire en 1530, à Cork, County Cork, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1574, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

Sie hat 1530 in Cork, County Cork, Irland Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1574 im Alter von 70 Jahren in London, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Cecily Beaumont(이)가 1504년 영국 레이스스터실르 오턴  테 힐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William I De Beaumont 2nd Viscount, KN(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Bassett(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1530년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크에서 Sir Henry Fenton of Nottinghamshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1574년 영국 런던에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.
